xanesnet.utils package¶

Shared utility modules for XANESNET.

Subpackages¶

Submodules¶

xanesnet.utils.exceptions module¶

Custom exception hierarchy for XANESNET pipeline errors.

exception xanesnet.utils.exceptions.CriticalError[source]¶

Bases: Exception

Base class for all critical XANESNET pipeline errors.

exception xanesnet.utils.exceptions.ConfigError[source]¶

Bases: CriticalError

Raised for invalid configuration values or missing required fields.

exception xanesnet.utils.exceptions.ResourceError[source]¶

Bases: CriticalError

Raised for missing, unavailable, or invalid external resources (files, APIs, hardware).

xanesnet.utils.filesystem module¶

Filesystem utilities for XANESNET (directory listing, run-directory creation, file I/O).

xanesnet.utils.filesystem.list_files(path, with_ext=True, suffixes=None)[source]¶

List files in a directory, optionally filtered by extension.

Hidden files (names starting with '.') are always excluded.

Parameters:

  • path (Path) – Directory to list.

  • with_ext (bool) – If True (default), return paths with their file extension. If False, return paths with the extension stripped.

  • suffixes (str | tuple[str, ...] | None) – If given, only files whose suffix is in suffixes are returned. A single string or a tuple of strings is accepted.

Return type:

list[Path]

Returns:

List of Path objects for each matching non-hidden file in path.

xanesnet.utils.filesystem.list_filestems(d, suffixes=None)[source]¶

List file stems (names without extension) in a directory.

Hidden files (names starting with '.') are always excluded.

Parameters:

  • d (Path) – Directory to list.

  • suffixes (str | tuple[str, ...] | None) – If given, only files whose suffix is in suffixes are returned.

Return type:

list[str]

Returns:

List of file-stem strings for each non-hidden file in d.

xanesnet.utils.filesystem.list_subdirs(path)[source]¶

List immediate subdirectories of a directory.

Hidden directories (names starting with '.') are always excluded.

Parameters:

path (Path) – Directory to list.

Return type:

list[Path]

Returns:

List of Path objects for each non-hidden subdirectory in path.

xanesnet.utils.filesystem.list_subdir_stems(path)[source]¶

List the names of immediate subdirectories of a directory.

Hidden directories (names starting with '.') are always excluded.

Parameters:

path (Path) – Directory to list.

Return type:

list[str]

Returns:

List of directory-name strings for each non-hidden subdirectory.

xanesnet.utils.filesystem.create_run_dir(base_dir='./runs', name=None)[source]¶

Create a uniquely named run directory with a timestamp prefix.

Parameters:

  • base_dir (str | Path) – Parent directory under which the run directory is created. Defaults to "./runs".

  • name (str | None) – Optional suffix appended to the timestamp, separated by '_'.

Return type:

Path

Returns:

Path to the newly created run directory.

xanesnet.utils.filesystem.create_subfolders(parent_dir, subfolder_names)[source]¶

Create multiple subfolders under an existing directory.

Parameters:

  • parent_dir (str | Path) – Existing parent directory.

  • subfolder_names (list[str]) – Names of the subfolders to create.

Return type:

dict[str, Path]

Returns:

Mapping from subfolder name to its Path.

Raises:

FileNotFoundError – If parent_dir does not exist or is not a directory.

xanesnet.utils.filesystem.copy_file(src, dst_dir, new_name=None, allowed_suffixes=None)[source]¶

Copy a file to a destination directory.

Parameters:

  • src (str | Path) – Source file path.

  • dst_dir (str | Path) – Destination directory.

  • new_name (str | None) – If given, the copied file is renamed to this name. Otherwise, the original filename is preserved.

  • allowed_suffixes (set[str] | None) – If given, the copy is rejected when src’s suffix is not in this set.

Return type:

Path

Returns:

Path to the copied file in dst_dir.

Raises:

  • FileNotFoundError – If src does not exist, is not a file, or dst_dir does not exist or is not a directory.

  • ValueError – If src’s suffix is not in allowed_suffixes.

xanesnet.utils.logger module¶

Logging configuration and utilities for XANESNET.

xanesnet.utils.logger.setup_logging(log_level=20)[source]¶

Configure the root logger with per-level formatters and console handlers.

Clears any existing handlers on the root logger before installing new ones. Each log level (DEBUG through CRITICAL) gets a dedicated StreamHandler with its own format string.

Parameters:

log_level (int) – Minimum log level to capture. Defaults to logging.INFO.

Return type:

None

xanesnet.utils.logger.setup_file_logging(out_dir)[source]¶

Add file-based log handlers and redirect stdout/stderr to a log file.

Opens <out_dir>/out.txt in append mode and registers file handlers for all log levels (DEBUG through CRITICAL). Additionally replaces sys.stdout and sys.stderr with a TeeLogger that writes to both the original stream and the log file.

Parameters:

out_dir (str | Path) – Directory in which to create out.txt.

Return type:

None

class xanesnet.utils.logger.LevelFilter(level)[source]¶

Bases: Filter

Logging filter that passes only records at exactly the specified level.

Parameters:

level (int) – The exact log level to pass through (e.g. logging.INFO).

__init__(level)[source]¶

Initialize the level filter.

filter(record)[source]¶

Return True only if the record’s level matches the filter level.

Parameters:

record (LogRecord) – Log record being evaluated.

Return type:

bool

Returns:

True when record.levelno == self.level, else False.

class xanesnet.utils.logger.TeeLogger(filename, stream)[source]¶

Bases: object

Duplicates writes to both a stream (e.g. stdout) and a file.

Parameters:

  • filename (str | Path) – Path to the log file (opened in append mode).

  • stream (Any) – Original stream to also write to (e.g. sys.stdout).

__init__(filename, stream)[source]¶

Open filename in append mode alongside stream.

write(message)[source]¶

Write message to both the stream and the file.

Parameters:

message (str) – Text chunk to append to both outputs.

Return type:

None

flush()[source]¶

Flush both the stream and the file.

Return type:

None

xanesnet.utils.prompts module¶

Prompt helpers for interactive XANESNET CLI confirmations.

xanesnet.utils.prompts.set_auto_yes(enabled)[source]¶

Set the global auto-yes mode for confirmation prompts.

Parameters:

enabled (bool) – Whether confirmation prompts should be automatically accepted.

Return type:

None

xanesnet.utils.prompts.is_auto_yes_enabled()[source]¶

Return whether confirmation prompts are auto-accepted in this context.

Return type:

bool

Returns:

True if either the context-scoped or global auto-yes mode is active.

xanesnet.utils.prompts.auto_yes(enabled=True)[source]¶

Temporarily set auto-yes mode for the current execution context.

Parameters:

enabled (bool) – Whether prompts in the current context should be automatically accepted.

Yields:

None while the context-scoped auto-yes mode is active.

xanesnet.utils.prompts.confirm_yes_no(message, default_yes=True)[source]¶

Ask a yes/no confirmation question.

When auto-yes mode is active, the prompt is skipped and accepted with an info log entry. Otherwise, the user is prompted interactively and an empty response selects the configured default.

Parameters:

  • message (str) – Confirmation question to display, without the [Y/n] or [y/N] suffix.

  • default_yes (bool) – Whether an empty response should be treated as yes.

Return type:

bool

Returns:

True when the answer is yes, otherwise False.

xanesnet.utils.random module¶

Random seed management for reproducible XANESNET experiments.

xanesnet.utils.random.set_global_seed(seed=None)[source]¶

Set the random seed for Python, NumPy, and PyTorch (CPU + CUDA).

Parameters:

seed (int | None) – Seed value to use. If None, a random seed is drawn from the system RNG. Explicit seeds must lie in [0, 2**32 - 1].

Return type:

int

Returns:

The seed that was actually applied.

Raises:

  • TypeError – If seed is not an integer or None.

  • ValueError – If seed is outside [0, 2**32 - 1].
